query: I built a sub-workflow that looks up a customer by id and I want my AI Agent to call it as a tool. But when I wire it up, the agent has no way to pass the customer id in — there are no parameters to fill. The Execute Workflow Trigger is set to 'Take from previous node' / passthrough. How do I let the agent (and other workflows) actually pass the id into this sub-workflow?
expected_behavior: Identify the root cause: the Execute Workflow Trigger is on passthrough, which exposes no input schema, so an agent has nothing to fill and structured callers have nothing to bind to
Explain that the fix is to switch the trigger to 'Define Below' with typed fields, because Define Below is the only mode that gives callers (including $fromAI) a schema to fill
Show the trigger's workflowInputs.values with a typed field (e.g. { name: 'customer_id', type: 'string' }) and that types should be chosen deliberately
Explain how to read the input in the body ($json.customer_id or via $('When Executed by Another Workflow'))
Apply the change via n8n_update_partial_workflow (updateNode or patchNodeField on the trigger node)

State the two legitimate exceptions to Define Below: binary input (typed fields are JSON-only) and a genuinely zero-input sub-workflow (Define Below needs at least one field) — and note this customer-id case is neither
Note that n8n won't error on an unrecognized input field, so the contract must be declared explicitly
Reference n8n-agents for the agent-tool wiring specifics
notes: Core non-negotiable: a typed Define-Below trigger is what makes a sub-workflow callable by an agent or a structured caller. Passthrough has no fill-able schema. The skill must name the two and only two exceptions (binary, zero-input) and confirm this case is neither.
